actionable_feedback: Add a CSV or plain-text export (one file per sheet) for the Decision_Logic and Final_Decision_Table that includes both computed values and the display formulas (e.g., columns: cell_ref, formula_text, computed_value). This enables independent recomputation without openpyxl.

In the workbook, add explicit per-pack cost breakdown columns in Final_Decision_Table: Rework_Cost, Retest_Cost, Reject_Cost, and Total_Estimated_Cost. Each must be formula-backed and reference Inputs! constants (no hard-coded numbers) so reviewers can trace cost calculations.

Expand the Sensitivity sheet to include broader threshold perturbations and aggregate outcomes: show how many packets would pass/warn/fail under each threshold scenario and the aggregated cost per scenario (sum of Total_Estimated_Cost).

Add a Verification sheet containing at least one worked example (showing referenced input cells, intermediate Decision_Logic cells, and final cost calculation) for one packet (preferably P2) so a human verifier can recompute end-to-end and confirm formulas map to memo statements.
Export a plain-text file listing key cell formulas (sheet, cell, formula) for Decision_Logic and Final_Decision_Table so graders can inspect formula text even if openpyxl is unavailable.

Update the quality_gate_memo.md to point to exact cell references used for the representative formulas and to document deterministic assumptions (e.g., rework modeled only for leak/balance; cap/IR are hard stops).
Ensure no macros or nonstandard features are required to view formulas; prefer standard Excel formulas compatible with openpyxl/xlsxwriter.
If feasible, include a short 'How to validate' checklist in the memo that instructs a human verifier to open the CSV/plain-text exports and recompute one packet to confirm outputs.
artifact_specific_comments: quality_gate_memo.md: Clear and well-structured; includes packet-level citations and representative formulas. Improve by adding exact cell coordinates for the listed formulas and by including the 'How to validate' checklist.

battery_eol_warranty.xlsx: Contains the required sheets and appears to include formula-driven logic created via stdlib XML/zip fallback. Provide CSV/plain-text extracts and a text export of formulas so automated previews are not required for verification.
feedback_summary: Baseline submission produced both required artifacts and a clear memo describing decision logic, representative formulas, and a final decision table. The model grader assigned semantic score ~0.95 and the verifier accepted the result with a single primary warning: the .xlsx could not be programmatically parsed in this environment (openpyxl unavailable), so cell-level verification was not performed. Provide small, machine-readable exports and explicit per-pack cost outputs to remove reproducibility gaps and to make the workbook independently verifiable.

extra_model_fields.notes: Addressing the exports and explicit per-pack cost columns will materially improve reproducibility and allow graders to achieve full confidence.
extra_model_fields.primary_limitation: openpyxl unavailable in grader environment prevented programmatic .xlsx parsing; grader relied on memo and trace.
extra_model_fields.recommended_action_for_verifier: Open attempts/baseline/[EXCLUDED_GENERATED_ARTIFACT] and recompute at least one pack end-to-end (Decision_Logic -> Final_Decision_Table) or request the CSV/plain-text exports of key sheets.

raw_llm_output.suggested_revision: Produce a revised attempt that (1) adds CSV/plain-text exports of Decision_Logic and Final_Decision_Table with formulas and computed values, (2) introduces explicit per-pack cost columns (Rework/Retest/Reject/Total) formula-linked to Inputs!, (3) expands Sensitivity to include aggregated outcome and cost summaries, (4) adds a Verification sheet with a worked example for one packet, and (5) updates the memo to reference exact sheet/cell locations and the deterministic assumptions. trace_specific_comments: Agent trace documents a reasonable fallback (stdlib XML/zip) used to construct a true .xlsx when openpyxl/xlsxwriter were unavailable. Trace also records earlier syntax errors and retries - include a short note in the memo under Meta explaining the dependency-free generation approach and confirming no macros were used.
